description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular brick structure with a gabled roof covered in gray shingles, attached to a large open-sided shelter with a similar shingled roof. The main building features a single wooden door accessed by a small set of concrete steps, and a single rectangular window on the side. The open shelter area is supported by wooden posts and has a concrete floor, providing ample covered space for livestock or storage. The roof over the shelter is slightly lower and extends outward, with a visible transition in shingle color. The brickwork is uniform and the overall construction appears sturdy and utilitarian, suitable for agricultural use. There are no visible mechanical ventilation features, but the open-sided shelter allows for natural airflow.
description: A sturdy brick cowshed with a gabled shingle roof, attached to an open-sided shelter supported by wooden posts and a concrete floor. Features a single wooden door, one window, and ample covered space for livestock.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main brick building with a wooden door, concrete steps, and the open shelter area to the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open shelter with wooden posts, the concrete floor, and a single window on the brick wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the building with no visible doors or windows, and the two distinct roof sections.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the gabled shingle roofs and the open shelter area beneath.
attribute_door_style: single wooden plank door with vertical panels
attribute_window_presence: one rectangular window on the side
